steps.title: Match the river subject
steps.content: The subject is "The Kolyma River," which is singular. The relative clause "which is 1,317 miles long" is extra information and does not affect agreement. The sentence describes a stable geographic fact, so a singular simple present verb is needed.

steps.title: Eliminate agreement errors
steps.content:
• Choice B: "Are flowing" is plural and progressive, so it does not fit the singular subject or permanent fact.
  • Choice C: "Flow" is plural in this context.
  • Choice D: "Have flowed" is plural present perfect and does not match the sentence's meaning.
The answer is A.
